title: Hyparrhenia papillipes
intro: Hyparrhenia papillipes is a type of grass found in tropical and subtropical regions, scientifically known as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hyparrhenia papillipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hyparrhenia papillipes is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used in traditional remedies such as infusions and poultices to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Hyparrhenia papillipes is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae.
